title: The Aloe
content:
My life was like an Aloe flower, beneath an orient sky,
    Your sunshine touched it for an hour; it blossomed but to die.

    Torn up, cast out, on rubbish heaps where red flames work their will
    Each atom of the Aloe keeps the flower-time fragrance still.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Laurence Hope (Adela Florence Cory Nicolson), titled "The Aloe", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
